name: scenario_PreviewDriver_detail
description: Toggle the Preview driver's detail and decompress controls on a running system and measure the render-FPS impact. detail 2/3 have a known, accepted downsample cost on the render task; decompress is purely client-side and cannot affect the render tick (see performance.md). All steps assert a relative bound (min_pct) only — a single ESP32 scenario step swings too much for an absolute FPS floor to be meaningful (the absolute throughput floor is enforced in collect_kpi.py --commit, which uses a settled reading). detail 3 gets a looser bound because its downsample cost is real and accepted.

steps.name: detail-1-coarse
steps.description: detail=1 (coarsest, 16x16 downsample on a 128 grid). Cheapest preview render.

steps.name: detail-2-medium
steps.description: detail=2 (medium, 32x32 downsample). Known accepted cost — must still hit 80% of baseline.

steps.name: detail-3-fine
steps.description: detail=3 (finest, 43x43 downsample). Looser bound (70%) because the downsample cost is real and accepted.

steps.name: decompress-on
steps.description: decompress=true. Client-side hint — must not affect the render tick.

steps.name: decompress-off
steps.description: decompress=false. Same as above — pure client-side, no render impact expected.
